title: Don't Duplicate Work - Keep THE SAME item on multiple boards!
excerpt:
Let’s cut to the chase. 
If you’re using monday.com and feeling frustrated by scattered updates, messy boards, and the never-ending struggle with mirror columns, I’ve got something that’s going to change the game for you…

posts.author: garfield-labs (Snir Alon)
posts.cookedHtml:
Let’s cut to the chase.

If you’re using monday.com and feeling frustrated by scattered updates, messy boards, and the never-ending struggle with mirror columns, I’ve got something that’s going to change the game for you.

Here’s the reality:

Mirror columns sound great in theory, but they come with serious limitations. You can’t use them for certain automations. They don’t show up in forms. And let’s not even talk about how some of your formulas can’t recognize them.

Managing multiple boards is a nightmare. Imagine each board on your workspace is a classroom, and every item on that board represents a student. Students, obviously, can participate in multiple classrooms. However - In monday, you have to duplicate & mirror that student across every board, leading to errors, data loss, and a lot of wasted time. It’s a mess.

But -

there’s a better way.

Check out the best-selling monday app Same Item Multiple Boards:

Easily set master views —Bring tasks from different boards into one master board, so you can oversee everything at a glance. No more jumping between boards to get a clear picture.

Drop the mirror columns —Say goodbye to the limitations and hello to seamless automations and integrations!

Keep your items consistent —Update once, and see it reflected across all boards. This is TRUE efficiency.

Now, here’s the kicker: You can try Same Item Multiple Boards for FREE. No risk, no hassle—just a better way to work.

So, why keep struggling? Click Here, give it a try, and experience what real productivity feels like!

posts.author: awright (Amanda)
posts.cookedHtml: I see that a status on the parent item can be updated on the master board. But what about subitem statuses? Can submitems be updated on the master board and then will update on the other boards?

posts.author: garfield-labs (Snir Alon)
posts.cookedHtml: Yup, subitems are updates as well. In fact, you can even take it a step forward and add subitems as regular items in another board: How to Add Sub-Items as Regular Items on Another Board | Same Item Multiple Boards

posts.author: JHarrington (Jen H)
posts.cookedHtml:
Good Day!
I have been fiddling around with this App and do like it. However, I have been trying to figure out how to get new items to show up on each of the two boards. When I do the automation, it acts like it is in a “loop” and keeps adding the same new item.
Example: Client Facing Board - client adds a new item and it should be added to the Internal Board. Same for the Internal Board.
When I set up the automation on each board, it just keeps adding the item to both boards over and over. Thoughts?
Thanks.

posts.author: garfield-labs (Snir Alon)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m not quite sure I understand the issue, but adding the same item more than twice to the same board isn’t possible with the app. This is why you might see the automation ran, but nothing happened. I’ll be happy to take a deeper look at it, you can book a call with us here: Snir Alayof - Same Item Multiple Boards Demo | TidyCal
